seoTitle: Thoona vs Thornton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Thoona and Thornton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thoona and Thornton.
heading: Thoona vs Thornton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thoona, Victoria and Thornton, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thornton has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Thoona (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thoona or Thornton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thoona has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Thornton scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thoona scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Thornton scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thoona or Thornton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thornton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
